$comment: What the stats monitor watches. It NEVER edits site data — it only alerts (via a GitHub issue) when a public source drifts from these baselines, so a human can verify in-app and update. When you confirm and update a real figure on the site, bump its baseline here too.

metrics.label: Spotify monthly listeners
metrics.note: Shown on the site as ~56.52M (app/data/africasBiggest.ts). Re-verify in the Spotify app before updating.

metrics.label: Spotify peak monthly listeners (all-time high)
metrics.note: A new all-time peak is a milestone worth logging on /updates. Auto-published hourly behind the sanity gate; a >12% jump (kworb mis-read) is rejected.

siteTargets.describes: Burna Boy's peak value on the monthly-listeners leaderboard

manualAfter: Add a new dated point to app/data/trends.ts (the daily series)
Log the new peak on /updates (app/data/updates.ts), newest-first
Refresh the leaderboard note wording if it crosses a round milestone

metrics.label: Global rank by Spotify monthly listeners
metrics.note: Burna Boy's position on kworb's global monthly-listeners ranking (lower is better). A move of 5+ places is worth a look.

metrics.label: Total Spotify streams (all songs)
metrics.note: Cumulative Spotify streams across every Burna Boy song — worth a milestone note as it climbs (e.g. crossing 11B).

metrics.label: Spotify followers
metrics.note: Official Spotify API (needs SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID/SECRET secrets). Auto-published hourly; the display string only changes when it crosses a 0.1M boundary.

siteTargets.describes: the displayed follower count on the leaderboard and stat cards

metrics.label: “Dai Dai” official video — YouTube views
metrics.note: Paul asked to always keep the Dai Dai video count current. The DAI_DAI_VIDEO_VIEWS constant drives the figure on /dai-dai.

siteTargets.describes: raw view count that /dai-dai formats to millions

manualAfter: If it crosses a round milestone (600M, 700M…), log it on /updates
